notes: Some species intentionally included without images yet.
image_optional suppresses automatic review-only placement for no-image entries.
Enriched with boleteGroup, boleteSubgroup, poreColor, and stemFeature to support bolete family-style filtering.
Adjusted explicit bolete image links for species reported as broken.

records.notes: Red-and-yellow bolete group. Added in the first 20-species bolete pack.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other red or staining boletes by checking pore color, whether blue staining is immediate or weak, cap color, stalk ornamentation, and whether the species is tied to a particular host tree.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these on the ground in hardwood or mixed forest, then verify the red or orange pore / cap features and whether the flesh stains blue when bruised or cut.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as good.

records.notes: Bright red cap and stem with yellow pore surface when young.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other red or staining boletes by checking pore color, whether blue staining is immediate or weak, cap color, stalk ornamentation, and whether the species is tied to a particular host tree.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these on the ground in hardwood or mixed forest, then verify the red or orange pore / cap features and whether the flesh stains blue when bruised or cut.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as edible with caution.

records.notes: Long-stemmed bolete in the broader king-bolete orbit.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other brown-capped boletes by checking whether the pores stay pale or yellowish, whether the flesh stains, whether the stalk is netted, and whether the taste stays mild rather than bitter.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these boletes on the ground in woods, often with hardwood or mixed forest associations, and check the whole mushroom rather than trusting a brown cap alone.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as good.

records.culinary_uses: Edible Suillus. Many foragers peel the slimy cap skin.
records.notes: Pine-associated Suillus with yellow tones.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other Suillus by checking whether the cap is sticky or dry, whether there is a ring or ring zone, the pore size and color, and the nearby conifer host.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these on the ground near conifers, especially pine or larch, often in sandy or needle-littered areas, and check for a sticky cap, pores rather than gills, and host-tree match.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as good. Verify the nearby tree association carefully, especially around Pine.

records.notes: Classic birch-associated scaber-stalk bolete.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other scaber-stalk boletes by checking the dotted / rough stalk surface, the host tree, cap color, and any slow bruising or graying in the flesh.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these on the ground near their preferred host trees, especially birch, aspen, or related hardwoods, and confirm the rough scabered stalk before trusting the identification.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as good. Verify the nearby tree association carefully, especially around Birch.

records.culinary_uses: Edible but not a top-tier bolete.
records.notes: Cracked cap surface can show reddish flesh tones. Image selection needs extra care because this complex is messy.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other red or staining boletes by checking pore color, whether blue staining is immediate or weak, cap color, stalk ornamentation, and whether the species is tied to a particular host tree.

records.notes: Included in the first bolete expansion as an additional edible Tylopilus candidate.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other Tylopilus by checking for pinkish pores, netting on the stalk, brown cap tones, and especially whether the flesh is mild or distinctly bitter.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these on the ground in forested settings, often among hardwoods, and verify pinkish pores or a bitter taste rather than assuming every brown bolete is edible.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as good.

records.notes: Dark shaggy bolete in the old-man-of-the-woods line.
look_alikes: None specifically documented in the current build yet
records.comparison_notes: Compare against other oddball boletes by checking the shaggy or dark cap / stalk texture, the pore surface, and the overall heavy, unusual build.
records.habitat_detail: Look for these on the ground in forest settings where the overall mushroom immediately looks strange, dark, shaggy, or unusually textured compared with cleaner, smoother boletes.
reviewReasons: Needs real look-alikes or explicit "none" review
records.edibility_detail: Current research layer rates this as good.
